findings.evidence: `LaplaceRepresentation.lean:80` defines `IsClosedCompletelyMonotone f := ContinuousOn f (Ici 0) ∧ IsCompletelyMonotoneOnIoi f`; `Basic.lean:273` is the existing home of `IsCompletelyMonotoneOnIoi`, and grep shows the closed predicate API is all in `LaplaceRepresentation.lean:80-194`.
findings.fix: Move `IsClosedCompletelyMonotone` and its basic lemmas (`continuousOn`, `isCompletelyMonotoneOnIoi`, `of_isCompletelyMonotone`, order lemmas, `shift_pos`, `congr`) to `CompletelyMonotone/Basic.lean` near `IsCompletelyMonotoneOnIoi`, or to a small early predicate file imported before the Laplace API.
findings.issue: `IsClosedCompletelyMonotone` and its namespace API are basic predicate material depending only on `ContinuousOn` and `IsCompletelyMonotoneOnIoi`, but they live in `LaplaceRepresentation.lean` behind measure/integration imports.

summary: The imports look purposeful, but the new closed-half-line complete-monotonicity predicate and its basic API are placed behind the Laplace-representation/measure file instead of with the core completely-monotone predicates.
